choice: All mutations are household-scoped Next server actions in lib/actions/* that go through requireHousehold() (resolves session -> householdId, scoping every query/where-clause so a member can't touch another household). Forms are progressive-enhancement <form action={serverAction}> with zod validation + revalidatePath; ownership edited by replace (delete asset_owners then re-insert). Year-availability is captured in the UI as a single {startYear,endYear|null} range + comma-separated excludeYears, persisted to the full Availability jsonb. Balance Snapshots: manual entry and CSV import both write a dated append-only batch (shared batchId, source manual|csv). CSV import uses a tolerant parser (lib/csv.ts: keyword-detected account+value columns, currency parsing) and matches rows to assets by case-insensitive name (exact then contains); unmatched accounts are reported as skipped, not errors. Asset current value = manualValue for manual assets, latest snapshot for synced.
rationale: [build-originated] The schema decisions (a5edec0f, b6012bc6, b1ee4eca) and screens (99d5cbfb) defined the entities and surfaces but not the write-path contracts: household-scoping via session, the single-range availability UI compromise, and the tolerant name-based CSV matching (E*TRADE export format is unspecified, so match by asset name rather than a fixed layout). A future builder extending CRUD or sync needs these.
